summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a high‑energy Carrier Dome show highlighted by Far From Me, Playing > Crazy Fingers and an epic Iko Iko
review: The recording is a pristine SBD with only a faint hiss, giving listeners a clear view of the band’s tight playing inside the cavernous dome.  The concert shines on a smoking second set – Far From Me opening into a seamless Playing > Crazy Fingers, a spacey Iko Iko/Truckin' transition and standout moments like Birdsong, Looks Like Rain, China > Rider and Brokedown Palace.  Energy stays high throughout, despite occasional acoustic quirks of the venue.

ai_rating.rationale: Average rating ≈4.1 from 21 reviews, overwhelmingly positive comments on both performance and sound quality; consistency across many detailed reviews gives high confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear SBD with a small but inoffensive hiss; drums sit high in the mix, overall balance is superb despite the dome’s acoustics.

standout_songs: Playing in the Band > Crazy Fingers
poor_songs: Iko Iko (transition to Truckin' considered a bit awkward by one reviewer)
show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong first set with Jack Straw, Birdsong and China>Rider; second set launches with Far From Me and flows through Playing/Crazy Fingers, Throwing Stones and an expansive jam before the encore. Transitions are praised as smooth and purposeful.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly noted for soaring guitar leads on Playing, Crazy Fingers and overall grand solos; even his vocal moments were highlighted.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as bubbling/bombing bass with massive low‑end power; praised especially in China>Rider and the closing jam.
band_member_comments.Bob: Lyrical anomalies on Looks Like Rain noted, plus strong vocal contributions throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s electric piano singled out as fantastic – “fantastic take on Far From Me” and overall keyboard brilliance.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums are high in the mix, tight and powerful; the post‑drum space is called “spacey” and a highlight of the show.
